Title: Metabolo-Transcriptomics Analyses Reveal Alfalfa Adaptation to Combined Saline-Alkali and Low-Temperature Stress in the Field

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Zhaodong; Blue Moon; WT and MsBAM1-RNAi transgenic alfalfa
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes alpha-1,4-glucosidic bonds in starch from the non-reducing ends to release maltose, promoting soluble sugar accumulation during stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Osmoprotection, lowering cytoplasmic freezing point, stabilizing cytoplasmic structures, enhancing energy metabolism and stress resistance under combined saline-alkali and low-temperature stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes soluble sugar accumulation and stress tolerance; RNAi knockdown lowers beta-amylase activity and soluble sugar content, increases starch, oxidative damage and chlorophyll fluorescence injury under combined stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under combined saline-alkali and low-temperature stress; higher in ZD than BM; identified as a WGCNA hub gene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root, especially under October cold acclimation and December freezing stress in saline-alkali soil; higher in ZD than BM
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Combined saline-alkali and low-temperature stress in field roots; validation under -1 degrees C plus saline-alkali irrigation for 7 days in pot experiment
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported as pivotal for starch degradation-driven sugar accumulation under combined stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA hub identification; RNA-seq expression profiling; metabolo-transcriptomic integration; gene cloning; RNAi knockdown in alfalfa; beta-amylase activity assay; soluble sugar and starch measurements; stress phenotype and chlorophyll fluorescence analyses
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsBAM1 was identified as a top hub gene associated with core carbohydrate metabolites under combined stress. 2. MsBAM1 expression was induced more strongly in resistant ZD than in sensitive BM. 3. Silencing MsBAM1 reduced beta-amylase activity and soluble sugar content, increased starch accumulation and stress injury, demonstrating a causal role in combined stress tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering or breeding alfalfa with improved tolerance to combined saline-alkali and low-temperature stress by enhancing starch-to-sugar conversion capacity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi lines showed impaired photosynthetic performance and stronger oxidative damage under stress; no obvious phenotype difference under normal conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: SRA PRJNA1261659; untargeted metabolomics: MetaboLights MTBLS12611
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the only hub gene functionally validated by transgenic analysis in the paper and the primary experimentally confirmed causal gene-metabolite relationship.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Starch and sucrose metabolism; trehalose biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes trehalose-6-phosphate formation in the trehalose biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Osmoprotection and stabilization under abiotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in resistant ZD is associated with increased trehalose accumulation under combined stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; higher under combined stress, especially in ZD
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key gene in trehalose synthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; WGCNA hub identification; integrated metabolomics-transcriptomics correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TPS1 was one of the top 10 hub genes. 2. Trehalose-related metabolites accumulated more strongly in ZD than BM under combined stress. 3. Higher TPS1 expression is associated with enhanced stress-related carbohydrate accumulation in ZD.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving trehalose-mediated osmoprotection under combined stress

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Galactose metabolism; raffinose family oligosaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes galactinol formation to support raffinose family oligosaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in ZD is associated with elevated raffinose accumulation under combined stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; induced under combined stress, with stronger expression in ZD
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key gene in raffinose synthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; WGCNA hub identification; integrated metabolomics-transcriptomics correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GolS2 was identified as a hub gene correlated with core metabolites. 2. ZD showed higher GolS2 expression and higher raffinose accumulation than BM. 3. The gene likely contributes to combined-stress tolerance via raffinose pathway activation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for boosting raffinose-mediated protection in stress-resilient alfalfa

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Starch and sucrose metabolism; starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ADP-glucose production for starch biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Starch-related carbohydrate pool affecting soluble sugar availability
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports carbohydrate reserve formation and root development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in ZD suggests coordinated regulation of starch synthesis and turnover under combined stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; higher in ZD than BM under combined stress
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Controls starch content according to discussion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; WGCNA hub identification; metabolite-gene coexpression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AGPS1 was a top hub gene under combined stress. 2. It is proposed to regulate starch content and root development. 3. Higher expression in ZD indicates coordinated starch metabolism contributes to stress adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning starch reserve dynamics together with starch degradation genes

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Galactose metabolism / cell wall galactan synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Interconverts UDP-glucose and UDP-galactose, supporting galactan and cell wall-associated carbohydrate synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Alters cell wall mechanical properties and enhances cold resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in ZD suggests contribution to cell wall modification and combined-stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; higher in ZD than BM under combined stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGE1 was identified as a hub gene. 2. Its elevated expression in ZD is associated with enhanced carbohydrate/cell-wall related adaptation. 3. The gene is proposed to contribute to cold resistance through galactan synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving structural stress tolerance through cell wall carbohydrate remodeling

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor; ABA-responsive element-binding factor
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes accumulation of raffinose, trehalose, glucose and proline in resistant ZD under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; identified as hub gene under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; combined drought/cold cited in discussion; inferred combined stress relevance here
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes involved in raffinose, trehalose and proline biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linked to osmoprotectant accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABF2 was one of the top 10 hub genes associated with core metabolites. 2. It is proposed to mediate ABA-dependent regulation of osmoprotectant accumulation. 3. Increased sugars and proline in ZD are consistent with ABF2 involvement in combined-stress adaptation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for multi-metabolite stress resilience engineering

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant ROS scavenging and stress resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes flavonoid accumulation in ZD under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; hub gene in metabolite-associated module
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CHS; CHI; PAL and flavonoid biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate positive regulator of antioxidant flavonoid accumulation under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA hub identification; RNA-seq/metabolite association; inference from known MYB2 function discussed by authors
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB2 was identified as a hub TF associated with core metabolites. 2. The authors propose that it regulates expression of flavonoid biosynthesis genes under combined stress. 3. This likely contributes to higher flavonoid accumulation in ZD than BM.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for enhancing flavonoid-based oxidative stress protection

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cold-responsive transcriptional regulation linked to osmotic regulator accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to help ZD rapidly respond to low temperature changes and accumulate osmotic regulators in roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; hub gene under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of cold adaptation-associated metabolite accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RVE5 was identified among the top 10 hub genes. 2. The authors infer a role in rapid low-temperature response. 3. It may support osmotic regulator accumulation contributing to combined-stress tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for improving cold-associated adaptive metabolism

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cold-responsive transcriptional regulation linked to osmotic regulator accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to help ZD rapidly respond to low temperature changes and accumulate osmotic regulators in roots
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; hub gene under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of cold adaptation-associated metabolite accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RVE7 was identified among the top 10 hub genes. 2. The authors cite related evidence linking RVE genes to cold-induced gene activation. 3. RVE7 is proposed to contribute to osmoprotectant accumulation and combined-stress resistance in ZD.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for breeding improved cold and combined stress resilience

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher NPR1 expression in ZD indicates more active SA signaling under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; significantly upregulated in ZD in October
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: SA-dependent signal transduction and transcriptional co-regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PR genes; TGA-associated defense transcriptional outputs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes PR gene expression in SA pathway context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key SA-dependent signaling node associated with stronger stress-responsive defense state in ZD
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq pathway profiling; integrated transcriptome-metabolome analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two NPR1 genes were significantly upregulated in ZD under combined stress. 2. Their expression coincided with stronger SA accumulation/signaling in ZD. 3. NPR1 is proposed to cooperate with TGA TFs to induce PR genes and enhance combined-stress resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to strengthen SA-mediated stress defense signaling

Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: TGA1: MS.gene88455; TGA3: MS.gene43041; MS.gene73963; TGA9: MS.gene35407; MS.gene28123; TGA10: MS.gene30742
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SA signal transduction / PR gene induction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in ZD suggests stronger SA-responsive defense regulation under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; significantly higher in ZD by December
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely activate PR gene expression with NPR1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent SA signaling regulators more active in resistant ZD
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq pathway profiling; integrated transcriptome-metabolome analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Several TGA TF genes were expressed at higher levels in ZD than BM under combined stress. 2. Their induction supports a more active SA signal transduction pathway in ZD. 3. The authors propose that NPR1 and TGA factors together enhance PR expression and stress resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulators for reinforcing SA-dependent defense under combined abiotic stress

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as a hub gene likely involved in combined-stress signaling contributing to ZD resistance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root; hub gene under combined stress
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CBL1 (based on function cited in discussion)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Stress-responsive signaling regulator rather than direct metabolic controller
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CIPK7 was among the 10 hub genes. 2. Based on known CIPK7 biology, the authors associate it with cold-response signaling. 3. It may indirectly support metabolic adaptation under combined stress.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream signaling target to improve combined-stress responsiveness
